rooms.name: Dark Abyss
rooms.description: The ground falls away into nothing. You stand at the edge of an absence that extends beyond sight. The air does not move; it waits. Below, shapes coil in geometries that should not exist, flickering at the edges like corrupted pixels.

rooms.name: Shadowlands
rooms.description: A flat, featureless expanse stretches in every direction. The ground is dark glass reflecting a sky with no stars. Your footsteps produce no echo. Occasionally, your reflection moves independently of you, skipping like a broken frame.
rooms.description_cleared: The shadowlands remain empty after the wraith falls. Your reflection still lags, but it no longer smiles first.

rooms.name: Graveyard
rooms.description: Headstones arranged in perfect concentric circles. Each bears a name you almost recognize. The dates are wrong. Some have not happened yet. The earth between them is freshly turned, as though the occupants have only recently arrived. Or recently left.
rooms.dev_note: // something keeps adding rooms when I'm not editing

rooms.name: Evil Fort
rooms.description: A structure that was not built but grown. Walls of dark iron twist upward at angles that hurt to follow with your eyes. There are windows, but they look inward, not out. The gate stands open. It has always been open. It was made for entering, not leaving.
rooms.dev_note: // I don't remember making this fort

rooms.name: Shadow Gate
rooms.description: A gate made of condensed shadow pulses with a rhythm that matches your heartbeat exactly. When you approach, it adjusts. When you retreat, it follows. Something valuable is hidden near the frame, but the gate feels like it wants a price for noticing.

rooms.name: Oblivion Gate
rooms.description: The gate is a tear in the fabric of the program. Through it, raw data scrolls past: character codes, memory addresses, and the scaffolding of the world laid bare. The guardian stands before it, assembled from pieces of deleted content.
rooms.description_cleared: The Oblivion Guardian has collapsed into broken symbols. Beyond the gate, the raw data still scrolls, but one object remains painfully solid: the dark crown waiting where the guardian stood.
rooms.dev_note: // this wasn't in my storyboard. how is it here?

rooms.name: Evil Dimension
rooms.description: Reality is thin here. The walls breathe, and the floor remembers each step, filling the impressions slowly with dark static. The large potion nearby feels less like treasure and more like a last chance. Ahead, the King's stronghold waits.
rooms.dev_note: // the evil dimension is getting bigger every time I open the file

rooms.name: Evil Stronghold
rooms.description: The throne room of the Evil King is vast and precise, every surface reflecting absolute darkness. The obsidian throne holds a figure that has been waiting. Not for you specifically. For anyone. For the next host. If you carry the crown, it grows warm here.
rooms.description_cleared: The throne is empty. The obsidian is just stone now, dull and ordinary. Whatever was waiting here is gone. The darkness in the corners feels like just shadow. You stand in front of the empty throne and the room is so quiet you can hear your own breathing. It is over.
rooms.dev_note: // the final boss. i didnt make him this strong. something changed the stats.
